input.subtitle_text:
1
00:05:22,100 --> 00:05:28,400
well the thing is nobody really understood what was happening until it was too late and by then the damage was already done and there was nothing anyone could do

2
00:05:28,800 --> 00:05:33,200
i mean we tried we really tried but when you're dealing with a system that is fundamentally broken from the inside

3
00:05:33,500 --> 00:05:36,100
there's only so much one person can do you know

4
00:05:40,000 --> 00:05:46,500
the report said fifteen thousand people were affected fifteen thousand and that number keeps growing every year because nobody fixed the root cause

1
00:00:05,000 --> 00:00:10,000
for forty years she worked the same land her father had worked before her

input.content_type: Documentary interview — foreign language dialogue (German), English subtitles
input.context: Interview with a German railway engineer discussing infrastructure problems
input.subtitle_text:
1
00:12:44,200 --> 00:12:50,800
the problem with the deutsche bahn is not the trains themselves the trains are fine the problem is thirty years of not investing in the infrastructure beneath them

2
00:12:51,100 --> 00:12:57,400
when I started working for db in nineteen ninety two we had a maintenance schedule that was the envy of europe now we have bridges that should have been replaced ten years ago

3
00:12:57,800 --> 00:13:02,500
and the federal government says oh we will invest we will invest but the money never comes or it comes too late

4
00:13:03,000 --> 00:13:08,200
you know what the real scandal is the real scandal is that everybody knows this every engineer every planner every politician and still nothing changes
